XSetAttributesOnHeapAlloc

Sets the allocation attribute flags associated with a specified heap entry. Used with the memory allocation replacement functions (XMemAlloc, XMemFree, and XMemSize).

VOID XSetAttributesOnHeapAlloc(
    PVOID pBaseAddress,
    DWORD dwAllocAttributes
);

Parameters

pBaseAddress
[in] Specifies the starting address of the heap entry.
dwAllocAttributes
[in] An XALLOC_ATTRIBUTES value that specifies the attribute flags for the specified heap entry.

Return Values

This function does not return a value.

Remarks

XSetAttributesOnHeapAlloc sets the attribute flags associated with the specified heap entry. Four reserved bytes in the heap entry header are used to store the attribute information. The actual allocation status, size, and protection of the entry are not changed; the specified attribute flags are simply written to the reserved area.

A custom memory allocation routine (XMemAlloc) is responsible for storing allocation attributes along with allocations if the dwHeapTracksAttributes member of the XALLOC_ATTRIBUTES structure is specified when the heap entry is allocated. The allocation routine can use XSetAttributesOnHeapAlloc to store the attributes in the reserved area of a heap entry returned by HeapAlloc.

Requirements

Header: Declared in Xbox.h; for include use xtl.h.

See Also

XGetAttributesOnHeapAlloc, XMemAlloc, XMemFree, XMemSize, HeapAlloc, XALLOC_ATTRIBUTES
